title: NBA Daily Dish: May 14, 2026
subtitle: Your daily dose of NBA news, scores, and highlights!

items.headline: Memphis Grizzlies Forward Brandon Clarke Dies at 29
items.summary: The NBA community is mourning the tragic loss of Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke, who passed away at 29 years old. An autopsy is reportedly planned to determine the cause of death.

items.headline: LeBron to Warriors Rumors Heat Up for 2026 Offseason
items.summary: Speculation is swirling around a potential blockbuster sign-and-trade that could send LeBron James from the Los Angeles Lakers to the Golden State Warriors, pairing him with Stephen Curry.

items.headline: NBA Concludes Giannis Injury Dispute Investigation, Trade Talks Persist
items.summary: The NBA has concluded its investigation into the dispute between Giannis Antetokounmpo and the Milwaukee Bucks regarding his injury status, with no further action warranted. Meanwhile, trade rumors involving the two-time MVP to teams like Cleveland, Houston, or Boston continue to swirl ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.

items.headline: Cavs Edge Pistons in OT, Take 3-2 Series Lead
items.summary: The Cavaliers mounted a furious comeback from a 15-point deficit to defeat the Pistons in overtime, securing a crucial 3-2 lead in their Eastern Conference Semifinals series. James Harden led the charge with a playoff-best 30 points.

items.headline: Spurs Dominate Wolves in Game 5, Up 3-2
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs took a commanding 3-2 series lead over the Minnesota Timberwolves in the Western Conference Semifinals with a dominant 126-97 victory. Victor Wembanyama showcased his talent with a stellar double-double.

items.headline: Thunder Sweep Lakers 4-0, Advance to WCF
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder completed a dominant sweep of the Los Angeles Lakers, winning their Western Conference Semifinals series 4-0 and advancing to the Western Conference Finals.

items.headline: Knicks Sweep 76ers 4-0, Head to ECF
items.summary: The New York Knicks made quick work of the Philadelphia 76ers, sweeping their Eastern Conference Semifinals series 4-0 and securing their spot in the Eastern Conference Finals.

items.headline: Harden's Heroics Lead Cavs to OT Win
items.summary: James Harden dropped a playoff-best 30 points, grabbed 8 rebounds, and dished out 6 assists to power the Cavaliers past the Pistons.

items.headline: Cunningham's Monster Effort Not Enough for Pistons
items.summary: Despite a stellar performance of 39 points and 9 assists, Cade Cunningham and the Pistons fell short in overtime against the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Wemby Dominates in Spurs' Game 5 Victory
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama showcased his all-around game with 27 points, 17 rebounds, 5 assists, and 3 blocks in the Spurs' crucial win over the Timberwolves.

items.headline: Mitchell Chips In for Cavs
items.summary: Donovan Mitchell added 21 points for the Cavaliers in their overtime triumph against the Pistons.

items.headline: Strus Provides Spark from Deep
items.summary: Max Strus contributed 20 points for Cleveland, sinking 6 three-pointers to aid their comeback effort.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Notable Playoff Performances
items.summary: Though his Portland Trail Blazers are out of the playoffs, Israeli forward Deni Avdija had standout performances earlier this postseason, including a 41-point playoff debut on April 14, 2026, and a 30-point, 10-rebound, 5-assist game against the Spurs on April 20, 2026.

items.headline: Joel Embiid Trade Speculation Continues
items.summary: With the 76ers eliminated, trade rumors involving Joel Embiid are picking up steam, with the Trail Blazers and Lakers identified as potential landing spots.

items.headline: OG Anunoby Expected Ready for Eastern Conference Finals
items.summary: Good news for the Knicks faithful! OG Anunoby is reportedly on track to be ready for the start of the Eastern Conference Finals.

items.headline: Cavs Host Pistons in Pivotal Game 6
items.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers will look to close out their Eastern Conference Semifinals series at home against the Detroit Pistons in Game 6.

items.headline: Wolves Battle Spurs in Game 6
items.summary: The Minnesota Timberwolves will try to force a Game 7 when they host the San Antonio Spurs in Game 6 of their Western Conference Semifinals series.
